title: Aureline rebuild-cost hint record
description: Boundary schema for the rebuild_cost_hint_record frozen in docs/storage/workspace_storage_detail_contract.md. The hint freezes how the workspace-storage detail surface, the clear-data review preview, the low-disk banner expansion, and the support-bundle storage section explain what it costs to rebuild a class entry: the offline-rebuild-risk class (would removal force a network round-trip, a mirror or offline-bundle round-trip, an admin-signed-pack round-trip, or is the bytes authoritative and not rebuildable at all), the startup-impact class (does removal break a feature, slow first-open, slow first-query, slow first-build, or stay user-invisible), the provenance-continuity class (does the rebuild reconstruct from local truth, from signed source, break provenance until re-mirrored, or is provenance authoritative and irreplaceable), the closed inputs the rebuild needs to consume, and the rebuild-safety summary class (cheap_to_rebuild_safe_to_remove, expensive_to_rebuild_but_safe, impossible_to_rebuild_offline, dangerous_to_delete_authoritative). The hint mints no new rebuild_cost_class; it consumes that vocabulary verbatim from /artifacts/runtime/storage_classes.yaml. Schema rules pair the four summary classes with the offline-risk, startup-impact, and provenance-continuity classes so a surface cannot label an entry 'cheap to rebuild' while also declaring it not rebuildable after removal, and cannot label authoritative state with the same hint copy as a hot disposable cache. Adding a value to a closed vocabulary is additive-minor and bumps rebuild_cost_hint_schema_version; repurposing a value or relaxing the summary-class pairings is breaking and requires a new decision row.

rebuild_cost_hint_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the rebuild-cost hint payload shape. Current value is 1.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence. Raw payload bytes, raw paths, raw URLs, raw secret material MUST NOT appear.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures; never part of the runtime record.

offline_rebuild_risk_class.description: Closed set naming what the rebuild would need to reach if the entry were removed. safe_to_remove_offline means the rebuild reads only from on-device source and is admissible when fully offline; rebuild_requires_network_resync needs a network round-trip to the provider, index source, or release ring; rebuild_requires_mirror_or_offline_bundle needs a customer-operated mirror, a vendor-published mirror, or an offline-bundle ref to be present and trusted; rebuild_requires_admin_or_policy_signed_pack needs an admin-signed pack, a policy bundle, or a tenant-managed pack to be present and trusted; not_rebuildable_after_removal is the authoritative, no-rebuild posture for user-owned recovery state and admissible authoritative evidence.

startup_impact_class.description: Closed set naming the startup-impact class the user sees if the entry is removed and the workspace continues. no_user_visible_impact is the cleanest disposable; slower_first_open_until_warm names hot-cache rewarming; slower_first_query_until_reindexed names knowledge-cache reindexing; slower_first_build_until_prebuilt names prebuild / toolchain rewarming; feature_unavailable_until_rebuilt names a feature that simply does not function until the rebuild completes (an offline-only docs pack, an offline-only model pack, a policy bundle); not_applicable_authoritative_state is the authoritative posture where 'startup impact' is the wrong frame because the bytes are not rebuildable at all.

provenance_continuity_class.description: Closed set naming whether the rebuild preserves the entry's provenance trail. provenance_preserved_rebuild_from_local_truth describes a derived index whose rebuild is reproducible from on-device source and whose provenance carries forward; provenance_preserved_rebuild_from_signed_source describes a mirrored or imported pack whose rebuild reads from the signed source and carries the signer chain forward; provenance_breaks_until_resigned_or_re_imported describes a class whose removal would force a re-import or a re-mirror before provenance can be re-established (an admin-signed offline bundle that is not currently reachable); authoritative_provenance_irreplaceable describes user-owned recovery state and case-bound evidence whose provenance cannot be rebuilt at all; not_applicable_disposable_no_provenance describes a hot disposable cache whose provenance is not material to the user.

rebuild_input_class.description: Closed set naming each input the rebuild needs to consume. The list is enumerated as a closed set so a surface cannot describe a rebuild as 'happens automatically' when it actually requires a mirror, an offline bundle, or an admin-signed pack.

rebuild_safety_summary_class.description: Closed four-class summary the workspace-storage detail surface, the clear-data review preview, the low-disk drill expansion, and the support bundle render verbatim. cheap_to_rebuild_safe_to_remove is the disposable hot-cache posture; expensive_to_rebuild_but_safe is the rebuildable knowledge-cache or prebuild posture (slow but no provenance loss); impossible_to_rebuild_offline is the mirrored, offline-bundle, or policy-signed-pack posture where removal is unsafe under air-gapped or network-degraded conditions; dangerous_to_delete_authoritative is the user-owned-recovery-state and authoritative-evidence posture where generic clear is refused outright.

rebuild_inputs_required.description: Closed list of inputs the rebuild needs. Authoritative state declares exactly [no_input_authoritative_state]; disposable derived state declares at least one local input; mirror / offline / policy-protected entries declare the matching mirror / offline-bundle / policy-pack input.

allOf.description: Authoritative no-rebuild cost forces the dangerous-to-delete summary plus the not-rebuildable-after-removal offline risk plus the not-applicable startup impact plus the irreplaceable provenance class plus the no-input rebuild list.
Dangerous-to-delete summary requires authoritative no-rebuild cost and the matching authoritative offline-risk / startup-impact / provenance / inputs.
Not-rebuildable-after-removal offline risk forces dangerous-to-delete summary so an authoritative class cannot wear cheap or expensive copy.
Not-applicable-authoritative-state startup impact forces the dangerous-to-delete summary so an authoritative class cannot collapse into a generic 'no impact' label.
Irreplaceable-provenance class forces the dangerous-to-delete summary so user-owned authoritative state never carries disposable hint copy.
Cheap-to-rebuild summary requires safe-offline risk, low-impact startup, preserved-from-local-truth or no-provenance continuity, and a low rebuild cost.
Expensive-but-safe summary requires safe-offline or network-resync risk plus a slower-rebuild startup-impact class plus a preserved-provenance continuity class.
Impossible-to-rebuild-offline summary requires a mirror / offline-bundle / policy-signed-pack offline-risk class and a feature-unavailable-or-slower-startup impact class.
Network-resync offline risk requires the network-provider rebuild input.
Mirror or offline-bundle offline risk requires at least one mirror / offline-bundle rebuild input.
Admin-or-policy-signed-pack offline risk requires the policy-signed-pack rebuild input.
Authoritative no-input rebuild list cannot mix non-authoritative inputs.
